---
title: "ig data analysis"
author: "chloe long"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE} 
#organizing the dataset by cleaning up variable names
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(MASS)
library(broom)

reels <- readr::read_csv("instagram_data_science_template.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    caption_length = caption_length_characters,
    views_24h = x24h_views,
    views_7d = x7d_views
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(c(caption_length, views_24h, views_7d, likes, comments, shares, saves, follows, reposts),
                  ~ readr::parse_number(as.character(.))),
    hook_type = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_to_lower(hook_type)),
    hook_type = as.factor(hook_type),
    hook_type_grouped = dplyr::case_when(
      hook_type %in% c("routine", "trending", "unboxing") ~ "high_effort_hook",
      hook_type %in% c("my creative idea", "none") ~ "low_signal_hook",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    ),
    hook_type_grouped = relevel(as.factor(hook_type_grouped), ref = "low_signal_hook"),
    firstframe_grouped = dplyr::case_when(
      firstframe %in% c("face", "face and product") ~ "face",
      firstframe %in% c("product", "greenscreen") ~ "product_or_graphic",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    ),
    viral = if_else(skip_rate_pct < quantile(skip_rate_pct, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 1, 0)
  )
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
reels <- read.csv("instagram_data_science_template.csv")

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)

reels <- readr::read_csv("instagram_data_science_template.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    caption_length = caption_length_characters,
    views_24h = x24h_views,
    views_7d = x7d_views
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      c(caption_length, views_24h, views_7d, likes, comments, shares, saves, follows, reposts),
      ~ readr::parse_number(as.character(.))
    )
  )

names(reels)
sum(is.na(reels$views_24h))
sum(is.na(reels$views_7d))
sum(is.na(reels$caption_length))

reels %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(views_24h)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(experience_month, date_posted, hook_type, views_24h) %>% 
  head(10)


```

```{r}
#troubleshooting
library(stringr)

reels$hook_type <- str_trim(reels$hook_type)
reels$hook_type <- str_squish(reels$hook_type)
table(reels$hook_type)
levels(as.factor(reels$hook_type))

```


```{r}

# â”€â”€ 1. LOAD â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
reels <- read.csv("instagram_data_science_template.csv")

# â”€â”€ 2. RENAME (matching your EXACT column names) â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
reels <- reels %>%
  rename(
    views_24h      = "X24h.views",
    views_7d       = "X7d_views",
    caption_length = "caption_length..characters."
  )

# â”€â”€ 3. FIX TYPES â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
reels <- reels %>%
  mutate(
    length         = as.numeric(length),
    caption_length = as.numeric(caption_length),
    skip_rate_pct  = as.numeric(skip_rate_pct),
    views_7d       = as.numeric(views_7d),
    views_24h      = as.numeric(views_24h),
    hook_type      = as.factor(hook_type),
    firstframe     = as.factor(firstframe),
    audio_trend_YN = as.factor(audio_trend_YN),
    collab_YN      = as.factor(collab_YN),
    CTA_caption        = as.factor(CTA_caption),
    viral          = ifelse(views_7d >= 20000, 1, 0)
  )

# â”€â”€ 4. SANITY CHECK â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
nrow(reels)
colnames(reels)
table(reels$viral)
table(reels$firstframe)
table(reels$hook_type)
colSums(is.na(reels))

reels <- reels %>%
  mutate(across(c(views_24h, views_7d, caption_length),
                ~ as.numeric(gsub(",", "", as.character(.)))))

# confirm it worked
sum(is.na(reels$views_24h))
sum(is.na(reels$views_7d))

```

```{r}
reels <- reels %>%
  mutate(viral = if_else(skip_rate_pct < quantile(skip_rate_pct, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 1, 0))
# low skip rate = high retention = "viral-ish" behavior; adjust threshold/logic to match your actual definition
reels <- reels %>%
  mutate(
    hook_type_grouped = case_when(
      hook_type %in% c("trending", "unboxing", "routine") ~ "high_effort_hook",
      hook_type %in% c("my creative idea", "none") ~ "low_signal_hook",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    )
  )

```

```{r} 
#viral logistic regression model
reels <- reels %>%
  mutate(
    firstframe_grouped = case_when(
      firstframe %in% c("face", "face and product") ~ "face",
      firstframe %in% c("product", "greenscreen") ~ "product_or_graphic",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    ),
    hook_type_grouped = case_when(
      hook_type %in% c("my creative idea", "problem") ~ "original_angle",
      hook_type %in% c("trending", "unboxing") ~ "trend_driven",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    )
  )

viral_model <- glm(viral ~ hook_type_grouped + CTA_caption + firstframe_grouped + caption_length,
                    data = reels, family = "binomial")
summary(viral_model)

# check for separation again
library(broom)
tidy(viral_model)  # if SEs are huge again, switch to lasso below; smaller number = better

```

```{r}
#linear regression for lower skip rate based on caption length + hook type + first frame but grouped
reduced_model <- lm(skip_rate_pct ~ caption_length + hook_type_grouped + firstframe_grouped + CTA_caption, 
                     data = reels)
summary(reduced_model)
```

```{r}
full_model <- lm(skip_rate_pct ~ caption_length + hook_type + firstframe + CTA_caption + length, 
                  data = reels)

# backward stepwise, AIC-based
best_model <- step(full_model, direction = "backward")
summary(best_model)

# compare candidate models properly
AIC(full_model, best_model)
```



```{r} 
#follows linear regression model
follows_model <- lm(follows ~ hook_type_grouped + CTA_caption + firstframe_grouped, data = reels)
summary(follows_model)

library(MASS)
follows_nb <- glm.nb(follows ~ hook_type_grouped + CTA_caption + firstframe_grouped, data = reels)
summary(follows_nb)

follows_poisson <- glm(follows ~ hook_type_grouped + CTA_caption + firstframe_grouped,
                        data = reels, family = "poisson")

# overdispersion check: ratio should be >>1 if NB is the right call
sum(residuals(follows_poisson, type = "pearson")^2) / follows_poisson$df.residual

AIC(follows_poisson, follows_nb)  # lower AIC wins
```

```{r}
reels_retention <- reels %>%
  drop_na(length, caption_length, skip_rate_pct, hook_type, firstframe)

reels_viral <- reels %>%
  drop_na(length, caption_length, views_7d, hook_type, firstframe)
reels <- reels %>%
  mutate(hook_type = str_trim(str_to_lower(hook_type)),
         hook_type = as.factor(hook_type))
```

```{r}
# Add interaction term â€” does face opener matter MORE for short videos?
model_v2 <- lm(skip_rate_pct ~ length * firstframe,
               data = reels_retention)
summary(model_v2)

# Try log-transforming views since they're skewed
reels_retention <- reels_retention %>%
  mutate(log_views = log1p(as.numeric(views_7d)))

```

```{r}
sum(is.na(reels$views_24h))
max(reels$views_24h, na.rm = TRUE)

# Bar chart for Median 24h Views by Hook Type (ungrouped)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)

reels <- readr::read_csv("instagram_data_science_template.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    caption_length = caption_length_characters,
    views_24h = x24h_views,
    views_7d = x7d_views
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(c(caption_length, views_24h, views_7d, likes, comments, shares, saves, follows, reposts),
                  ~ readr::parse_number(as.character(.))),
    hook_type = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_to_lower(hook_type)),
    hook_type = as.factor(hook_type)
  )

max(reels$views_24h, na.rm = TRUE)

medianviews <- reels %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(views_24h)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(hook_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(median_views = median(views_24h, na.rm = TRUE), n = dplyr::n()) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = reorder(hook_type, median_views), y = median_views, fill = hook_type)) +
  ggplot2::geom_col() +
  ggplot2::coord_flip() +
  ggplot2::labs(title = "Median 24h Views by Hook Type", x = NULL, y = "Median Views") +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none")

medianviews  # preview before saving

ggsave(
  "Median Views (24h) by Hook Type.png",
  plot = medianviews,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)
```

```{r}
# Bar chart for average skip rate based on hook type
skipratehook <- reels_retention %>%
  group_by(hook_type) %>%
  summarize(avg_skip = mean(skip_rate_pct, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(hook_type, avg_skip), y = avg_skip, fill = hook_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Average Skip Rate by Hook Type",
       x = "Hook Type", y = "Avg Skip Rate (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

skipratehook  # preview it before saving

ggsave(
  "Skip Rate Based On Hook Type.png",
  plot = skipratehook,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)
```

```{r}
# Does reel length affect retention and skip rate
skiprate_length <- ggplot(reels_retention, aes(x = length, y = skip_rate_pct)) +
  geom_point(color = "#833AB4", size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#E1306C") +
  labs(title = "Does Reel Length Affect Skip Rate?",
       x = "Reel Length (seconds)", y = "Skip Rate (%)") +
  theme_minimal()

skiprate_length  # preview before saving

ggsave(
  "Reel Length vs Skip Rate.png",
  plot = skiprate_length,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

```


```{r}
#first frame type vs. skip rate
firstframeskip <- ggplot(reels_retention, aes(x = firstframe, y = skip_rate_pct, fill = firstframe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Skip Rate by First Frame Type",
       x = "First Frame", y = "Skip Rate (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave(
  "First Frame Type vs. Skip Rate.png",
  plot = firstframeskip,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

```
